Add first-insurance surcharge: if customer.firstInsurance === true (or similar flag), add 10% per item base premium to policy.

Add `firstInsurance?: boolean` to Customer type. When set true, add 10% of each item's base premium to policy premium.

Make sure the previously passing tests (which don't set firstInsurance) still pass — i.e., default behavior is no surcharge.

Look at how the spec test was activated - it likely passes `firstInsurance: true` in the customer object.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal code to make it pass.

The expected: 100 (sword base) + 10 (10% of 100) + 5 (fee) = 115. Minimal implementation:

content.text:
All 19 tests pass (including the new first-insurance surcharge test) and no previously passing tests were broken.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: 
- Added optional `firstInsurance?: boolean` to `Customer` type.
- Added a `firstInsuranceAdjustment` policy-level adjustment that adds 10% of the policy base premium when `customer.firstInsurance === true`, mirroring the structure of `loyaltyAdjustment`.
- Wired it into `quote` by summing it into `policyAdjustments`.

**Result**: All 19 active tests pass (33 todos remain).

**Approach**: This is minimal because:
- The base premium calculation (`policyBase`) was already computed for the loyalty path; we simply reuse it.
- The new adjustment follows the same shape as the existing `loyaltyAdjustment`, so structurally minimal.
- For the test's single-sword case: 100 (base) + 10 (10% surcharge) + 5 (fee) = 115. ✓
- Default behavior unchanged: when `firstInsurance` is absent/false, the function returns 0, so all prior tests still pass.

Files changed:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_13-03-01_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
